title: salix purpurea var. angustifolia
intro: Salix purpurea var. angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved purple willow, is a variety of willow tree that is native to Europe and Asia. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with slender branches and narrow leaves, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The bark of Salix purpurea var. angustifolia contains salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it useful for relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, improving digestion, and supporting cardiovascular health.
